easy.title: Create a 'About Me' Page with Well-Structured Paragraphs
easy.description: Build an About Me page that demonstrates proper paragraph usage. Include: a main heading, three paragraphs about yourself (background, interests, goals), a horizontal rule separating sections, an address with line breaks, and a quote from your favorite book using <blockquote>.
easy.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>About Me</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Write about yourself using proper paragraph elements -->
</body>
</html>
easy.solution:
About Me

My Background

Hi, I am a web development student who is passionate about learning HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. I started coding six months ago and have been hooked ever since. The ability to create something from nothing using just code is fascinating to me.

My Interests

When I am not coding, I enjoy reading science fiction novels, hiking in nature, and experimenting with photography. I believe that creativity flows across all disciplines, and my hobbies often inspire my approach to web design.

My Goals

My short-term goal is to master HTML and CSS, then move on to JavaScript and frontend frameworks. Long-term, I aspire to become a full-stack developer and build applications that make a positive impact on people's lives.

Contact

Jane Doe
123 Learning Lane
Codeville, Webland 10001
Email: jane@example.com

Favorite Quote

The only way to do great work is to love what you do.

— Steve Jobs

© 2026 Jane Doe

hard.title: Build a Multi-Column Typography Showcase
hard.description: Create a typography showcase page that demonstrates advanced paragraph usage. Include: a section with multi-column text (use CSS inline style column-count), a section demonstrating text-indent and first-letter styling, a section that uses word-wrap and overflow properties, a code block using <pre> with syntax highlighting simulation, and a section showing different text-align values (left, center, right, justify). Each section must use proper headings and paragraphs.
hard.starterCode:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Typography Showcase</title>
</head>
<body>
    <!-- Build a typography demonstration page -->
</body>
</html>
hard.solution:
Typography Showcase

Demonstrating advanced paragraph formatting and CSS typography.

Multi-Column Layout

This section demonstrates multi-column text layout using CSS columns. The content flows naturally from one column to the next, creating a newspaper-like reading experience. This is achieved with the column-count CSS property.

Multi-column layouts are useful for displaying large amounts of text in a compact, readable format. However, be careful not to make columns too narrow, which can hinder readability.

Research suggests that optimal line length for readability is between 45 and 75 characters. When using multi-column layouts, ensure each column stays within this range.

Text Indent & First Letter

This paragraph has an indented first line, created with the CSS text-indent property. This traditional typographic practice helps readers identify paragraph breaks, especially in dense text without visible paragraph spacing.

This paragraph demonstrates a drop cap effect. The first letter is enlarged and styled differently. This technique adds visual interest and is commonly used in books and magazines.

Text Alignment

Left Aligned

Left-aligned text creates a clean, natural reading flow. The left edge is straight, and the right edge is ragged. This is the default alignment for left-to-right languages.

Center Aligned

Center-aligned text is best used for headings, titles, and short blocks of text. It is harder to read for long passages because the starting position varies.

Right Aligned

Right-aligned text creates a straight right edge and ragged left edge. It is commonly used for captions, quotes, and right-to-left languages.

Justified

Justified text aligns both left and right edges by adjusting word spacing. While it creates neat blocks, it can produce uneven gaps with narrow columns.

Word Wrap & Overflow

This paragraph has a fixed width and demonstrates word-wrap: break-word. LongwordslikethiswillbeBrokentoPreventOverflow.

This text is truncated with ellipsis when it overflows the container width because it has white-space nowrap...

Code Display with <pre>

function formatText(element) {
    const text = element.textContent;
    return text.trim();
}

© 2026 Typography Showcase. Built with HTML and CSS.
